source: Public Scenario.com model, app, help, and blog pages scraped/searched 2026-04-26; no login-only app content captured.
status: complete_public_notes; 20 candidates cataloged. Direct public OmniHuman model page was not found, but OmniHuman remains noted via Scenario's lip-sync blog and plan list.

candidates.name: Kling AI Avatar 2 (Pro)
candidates.what_it_does: Generates a lifelike talking video from one static image plus an audio track, with precise lip-sync and facial animation.
candidates.possible_use: Fast Morgan speaking-head clips from a approved portrait and generated voiceover.
candidates.notes: Pro-plan model per Scenario plan list; best fit when we need a direct image+audio avatar rather than a full 3D rig.

candidates.name: HeyGen Avatar 4
candidates.what_it_does: Creates realistic talking-avatar videos from a photo and text prompt with built-in TTS voices.
candidates.possible_use: Alternative Morgan presenter workflow when we want turnkey photo-to-spokesperson output.
candidates.notes: Starter-plan model per Scenario plan list; useful benchmark against Kling Avatar.

candidates.name: Veed Fabric Lipsync 1.0
candidates.what_it_does: Turns static images into high-fidelity talking videos with audio-driven mouth movement and facial expressions.
candidates.possible_use: Compare against Kling Avatar for Morgan head-and-shoulders messages where mouth accuracy matters most.
candidates.notes: Pro-plan model per Scenario plan list; supports 480p/720p according to public page.

candidates.name: Sync-3 Lipsync
candidates.what_it_does: Professional-grade video lip synchronization that aligns audio with facial movement.
candidates.possible_use: Polish or redub Morgan clips after a video take already exists.
candidates.notes: Pro-plan model per Scenario plan list; good for production comparison passes.

candidates.name: Pixverse Lipsync
candidates.what_it_does: Synchronizes an audio track to a speaker's mouth movement in video for realistic dialogue.
candidates.possible_use: Stylized Morgan avatar/dialogue tests, especially if a painterly or cinematic look is desired.
candidates.notes: Scenario blog positions Pixverse as stronger for art-directed lip sync; Pro-plan video editing model.

candidates.name: Kling Lipsync
candidates.what_it_does: Applies accurate lip-sync to an existing video using a separate audio track while leaving the rest of the clip mostly untouched.
candidates.possible_use: Quickly re-time Morgan dialogue in an existing generated or composited clip.
candidates.notes: Scenario blog calls Kling the fast iteration option for short lip-sync lines; Pro-plan video editing model.

candidates.name: Add characters into your video
candidates.what_it_does: Workflow app that composites one or two uploaded characters naturally into a reference video using Kling V3 Omni Video.
candidates.possible_use: Drop Morgan into existing background footage or announcements without green-screen setup.
candidates.notes: Useful if Morgan needs to appear in a scene rather than as a cropped talking portrait.

candidates.name: Kling V3 Omni Video
candidates.what_it_does: Top-tier video generation with cinematic visuals, fluid motion, native audio generation, and multi-shot support.
candidates.possible_use: Broader Morgan motion/video scene tests beyond lipsync-only head shots.
candidates.notes: Listed on models index and used by the add-characters app; Pro-plan model per current plan list.

candidates.name: Tripo 3.1
candidates.what_it_does: Converts a single image into a textured, game-ready 3D asset with high-quality PBR textures and low-poly/high-detail options.
candidates.possible_use: Convert Morgan concept art into a draft 3D model for testing silhouettes, turntables, or downstream rigging.
candidates.notes: Free-plan 3D model per Scenario plan list; blog notes Multi View can use front/back/left/right for stronger 360 consistency.

candidates.name: Tripo 3.0
candidates.what_it_does: Generates high-fidelity 3D models from text, sketches, or images with PBR maps, quad mesh options, and face-limit controls.
candidates.possible_use: Baseline 3D generation benchmark for Morgan before paying for newer/higher-tier models.
candidates.notes: Free-plan model; public blog emphasizes clean, usable game/animation/AR outputs.

candidates.name: Hunyuan 3D 3.1 Pro
candidates.what_it_does: Tencent image-to-3D model with 1536³ resolution, hierarchical 3D-DiT carving, 4K PBR textures, and face-count controls.
candidates.possible_use: Higher-fidelity Morgan 3D starting point once the 2D identity/turnaround is locked.
candidates.notes: Starter-plan model per current plan list; public page says generation takes several minutes and supports 50k-1.5M polygon targets.

candidates.name: Hunyuan/HY 3D Pro family (Standard, Multiview, Sketch)
candidates.what_it_does: Family workflow for single-image, multi-view turnaround, or sketch-to-3D generation, with optimized mesh controls.
candidates.possible_use: Use Standard for a Morgan hero portrait, Multiview for model sheets, or Sketch for early avatar concept volume tests.
candidates.notes: Blog explains Hunyuan was renamed HY and emphasizes these outputs as starting points, not final production rigs.

candidates.name: Image to Rigged Low Poly Character
candidates.what_it_does: App chain that turns one 2D character image into an optimized 3D asset using Tripo 3.1, retopology, texturing, and rigging tools.
candidates.possible_use: End-to-end Morgan proof of concept from portrait/concept art to low-poly rigged character.
candidates.notes: Uses Tripo 3.1, Tripo Rigging 1.0, Tripo Retopology, Tripo 3.0 Texturing, Photoroom, Gemini, SeedVR2, and Scenario LLM.

candidates.name: Game 3D Assets - Concept to 3D Model
candidates.what_it_does: Workflow app that turns text prompts and style references into high-quality 3D game assets using Gemini 3.1 and Tripo 3.1.
candidates.possible_use: Generate Morgan props/accessories or alternate style-matched 3D avatar concepts.
candidates.notes: Less rig-focused than the low-poly character app, but useful for style-to-asset iteration.

candidates.name: 3D Model Auto-Rigging: Biped Character
candidates.what_it_does: Workflow app for adding professional bone skeletons and rigging to 3D biped models using Tripo Rigging 1.0.
candidates.possible_use: Rig an imported Morgan humanoid mesh after Tripo/Hunyuan generation.
candidates.notes: Directly aligned with biped avatar animation readiness.

candidates.name: Tripo Rigging 1.0
candidates.what_it_does: Automatically detects skeletons and applies bones, skin weights, and retargetable preset animations to 3D meshes.
candidates.possible_use: Turn Morgan 3D mesh into an animation-ready asset for walk/talk/gesture tests.
candidates.notes: Pro-plan model per current plan list; supports multiple rig types, not just bipeds.

candidates.name: Tripo Retopology
candidates.what_it_does: Converts complex meshes into cleaner, lower-poly geometry with face limits, quad mesh option, and texture baking.
candidates.possible_use: Clean up Morgan meshes before rigging or realtime rendering.
candidates.notes: Pro-plan model per current plan list; important if Hunyuan/Tripo output is too dense.

candidates.name: 2D Animation Rigging Sheet
candidates.what_it_does: Generates separated 2D character parts sheets for Spine, Live2D, Moho, or similar 2D rigging tools.
candidates.possible_use: Alternative Morgan Live2D-style path if full 3D is overkill for speaking avatar needs.
candidates.notes: Uses Gemini 3.1; useful for testing a lightweight animated-avatar pipeline.

candidates.name: Generate Turnarounds in Scenario
candidates.what_it_does: Guide for creating consistent front/side/back character views using prompt edits, Pixverse rotation capture, or custom-trained models with pose control.
candidates.possible_use: Preparation step before 3D Morgan generation, especially for Hunyuan/Tripo multiview inputs.
candidates.notes: Public blog recommends retouch/upscale and ControlNet/Pose-style structure to reduce identity drift.

candidates.name: How to Choose the Right Model(s)
candidates.what_it_does: Scenario decision guide for image, video, generative 3D, and texture model selection.
candidates.possible_use: Use as evaluation rubric for Morgan experiments: start simple, compare models, then move to specialized or trained models.
candidates.notes: Mentions testing Seedance/Kling/PixVerse for video and Hunyuan/Rodin/Multiview for 3D.

candidates.name: Current plan model availability
candidates.what_it_does: Plan-tier list of Scenario models across image, video, 3D, audio, and text.
candidates.possible_use: Subscription planning for Morgan avatar tests: identify whether Free, Starter, Pro, Max, or Enterprise is needed.
candidates.notes: Key subscription signal: Tripo 3.0/3.1 are Free; Hunyuan 3.1 Pro is Starter; Kling Avatar, Veed Fabric, Sync-3, Tripo Rigging, and Retopology are Pro.
